overview: Sultai Midrange in Pioneer leverages efficient one-mana removal and resilient creatures to stabilize early and outvalue opponents in the mid-to-late game. By filtering draws with Consult the Star Charts and applying immediate pressure with Shark Typhoon, the deck aims to control the board while maintaining card advantage. Fatal Push ensures that early threats are neutralized efficiently, allowing your larger threats to clear the path.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and efficient removal
Closing out games with Shark Typhoon's flying and direct damage potential
Stabilizing with resilient creatures and filtering into finishers via Consult the Star Charts

keep_criteria: Hands with Fatal Push and a one-drop creature
Hands containing Consult the Star Charts and early interaction
Hands with two removal spells and a Shark Typhoon
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches/shocks), at least one turn-1 interaction (Thoughtseize) or turn-2 removal (Fatal Push), and a threat or engine.
Prioritize keeping lands that can untap for casting Fatal Push or Thoughtseize on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any one-mana spells or early interaction
Hands with only high-cost creatures and no draw
Hands missing land drops for the first three turns
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, 3+ non-basic lands without fetches, or hands lacking early interaction/threats.

early_game: Play lands and cast Fatal Push to remove early aggressive threats
Use Consult the Star Charts to filter into better hands and draw cards
Deploy Shark Typhoon to establish board presence and apply pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize if available, or hold for interaction.
Turn 2: Deploy Fatal Push against aggro, play a threat (Tarmogoyf/Bloodtithe Harvester), or begin casting Consult the Star Charts.
Sequence lands by playing non-basics first, then fetch/shock as needed to maintain hand size above 3.
Aim to have a creature with power 4+ by turn 3 to enable Shark Typhoon and discounted Star Charts.
mid_game: Trade efficiently with removal while preserving card advantage
Use Shark Typhoon's ability to deal direct damage or block efficiently
Filter draws to find larger threats and additional interaction
use Consult the Star Charts to dig for answers when resources are low.
Manage your graveyard carefully—exploit it for value when safe, but respect graveyard hate.
Avoid overcommitting mana to a single threat without protection.
Cast Star Charts when you control a 4+ power creature to maximize card advantage.
Commit threats when you have interaction to clear blockers or when the opponent is tapped out.
late_game: Stabilize the board with resilient creatures and continued card draw
Leverage Shark Typhoon's flying to bypass blockers and close the game
Maintain pressure while outlasting opponents through superior resource management
Shark Typhoon (attacks for 4+ and draws cards), Consult the Star Charts (digs for win conditions or answers), and resilient threats like Tarmogoyf.
Secure the game by transitioning to card advantage when threats are blocked or by attacking with Typhoon when the opponent has no sweepers.
Go all-in when you hold card advantage, have interaction to clear blockers, or the opponent is tapped out/low on resources.
Commit when you can trade favorably or close the game in 1-2 turns.
Use Shark Typhoon to pressure when you know they lack board wipes.

key_cards.name: Consult the Star Charts
key_cards.notes: Cast early to dig for removal or finishers; prioritize keeping it in opening hands for consistency.

key_cards.name: Shark Typhoon
key_cards.role: Efficient threat and direct damage engine
key_cards.notes: Attack with it to deal 2 damage to the opponent if you control another creature; use its flying to bypass early blockers.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Hold up for combat tricks or use on opponent's turn to clear one-drops and two-drops efficiently; prioritize it in mulligans.

sequencing_tips: Hold Fatal Push for combat tricks to maximize value against aggressive decks
Cast Consult the Star Charts before playing threats to ensure you have answers ready
Use Shark Typhoon's direct damage ability when you have a clear path to victory or need to close out a game quickly
Consult the Star Charts is powerful but can strand you if you lack mana or need immediate answers
cast it when you have a clear plan and a 4+ power creature.
Shark Typhoon is vulnerable to sweepers but excels at closing games when unanswered.
Avoid overextending into sweepers, and always track graveyard hate.
Common mistake: overcommitting to a single threat without protection or ignoring hand size maintenance.
Practice recognizing when to pivot from midrange to control or aggression based on the opponent's resources.

matchups.advice: Prioritize keeping Fatal Push and early interaction; use Shark Typhoon to block and apply pressure while filtering into larger threats.
watch_for: Board wipes that clear your early board

matchups.advice: Play around counterspells by splitting your hand; use Shark Typhoon to apply pressure while they search for answers.
Race efficiently by leveraging card advantage; use Fatal Push to clear their early threats and Shark Typhoon to maintain board presence.

Use multiple cheap threats, discard counterspells with Thoughtseize, or go wide/small. Cast threats at end step or use instant-speed interaction to bypass counters. Force them to use multiple counters on different threats to exhaust their resources.

Early interaction (Thoughtseize, Fatal Push) to disrupt mana dorks. Discard ramp spells, stabilize with removal, and avoid overextending into their late-game finishers. Win the early game and transition to value before they stabilize.

Win the interaction war by using Thoughtseize to disrupt their engines. Win the card advantage race and sideboard in disruption/graveyard hate. Trade favorably and avoid overextending into their removal. Control the board state and force them to make the first mistake.

common_threats: Flying threats that bypass your blockers
Board wipes that reset the board state
Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Anger), counterspells, and graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Grafdigger's Cage).
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or discard their hand dry.
Track when they run out of interaction to apply pressure, and monitor their graveyard for recursion or combo pieces.
